title: limonium roridum
intro: Limonium roridum, also known as the rough sea lavender, is a shrubby perennial plant belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family, characterized by its small, fragrant purple flowers and greyish-green foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Limonium roridum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and can also be used in topical applications to take advantage of its soothing and anti-inflammatory effects.
